items.label: storefront-dev
messages: Now let me explore the existing codebase structure and reference files.
Now let me look at existing overrides for import patterns, and the shared UI module:
Good. Now let me check the test utility `renderWithProviders` and check what exists:
Now I have enough context. Let me check the existing directories and then create all files.
The current ProductTile override has positioning issues — the bar needs to be at the bottom of the image area, not the bottom of the whole tile. Let me fix it:
Now let me create the unit tests:
Now let me run the unit tests:

The `transformIgnorePatterns` only transforms `@salesforce/retail-react-app` but not `@salesforce/cc-datacloud-typescript`. I need to either add it or mock the problematic module. Let me mock the datacloud module and check if there are other issues:
I need to add `@salesforce/cc-datacloud-typescript` to the transform ignore pattern, or mock it. Let me update the test files to add the necessary mock:

The `design/react` subpath doesn't exist. I need to mock it. Let me also check for any other potential missing modules by creating comprehensive mocks. Instead of fighting with `renderWithProviders`, let me create my own lightweight render helper:
The `toBeInTheDocument` matcher isn't available. I need to import `@testing-library/jest-dom` or check the setup:
No `setupFilesAfterSetup` for `@testing-library/jest-dom`. I need to either import it in each test or use different assertions. Let me add the import:
I need to add `setupFilesAfterSetup` to our jest config, or import `@testing-library/jest-dom` in each test. Let me add it to both test files:
All 14 QuickViewModal tests pass with 100% coverage. Now let me run the ProductTile tests:
All 12 ProductTile tests pass. Now let me validate the dev server compiles correctly:
Dev server returns HTTP 200 — webpack compiled successfully and all modules resolve. Now let me check the SSR safety checklist and verify the validation hooks:
Now let me check if there's a validation hook file to update:

The feature doesn't create new routes or pages — it adds a Quick View modal to existing product tiles. No new route check needed. The spec only creates override components, not new page routes.  Now let me commit and complete the pipeline:

The `storefront-dev` phase for `product-quick-view` is complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:  ## Implementation Summary  ### Files Created (4 files)  | File | Description | |---|---| | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx` | QuickViewModal component — Chakra Modal wrapping ProductView with loading spinner, error state, and accessible aria-label | | `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js` | 14 unit tests — modal shell, content rendering, accessibility, focus management | | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx` | ProductTile override — wraps base tile with Quick View overlay bar using slide-up animation | | `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js` | 12 unit tests — bar rendering, interaction (preventDefault/stopPropagation), visual states |  ### Key Architecture Decisions  1. **Reused existing patterns**: `useProductViewModal` hook + `ProductView` component from the base template — no custom API fetching 2. **PWA Kit override mechanism**: Files in `overrides/app/components/` shadow the base template, base component imported via `@salesforce/retail-react-app/...` 3. **Image area positioning**: Used `paddingBottom: 100%` overlay trick to match the 1:1 AspectRatio image area, with `overflow: hidden` for clean slide animation 4. **SSR-safe**: No `window`/`document` access, `useDisclosure` defaults to `isOpen: false`, no hydration mismatches 5. **Corner cases handled**: Product sets/bundles hidden, missing productId guarded, error state for unavailable products  ### Verification Results - ✅ 26 unit tests passing (14 + 12), 100% line coverage - ✅ Dev server compiles and returns HTTP 200 - ✅ SSR safety checklist passed - ✅ All `data-testid` attributes in place for E2E testing

items.label: create-draft-pr
messages: Now let me get the branch name and check for existing PRs, then create the draft PR.
Good — no existing PR and branch is `feature/product-quick-view`. Let me now create the draft PR.
Draft PR created successfully at **https://github.com/RomanKaliupinMelonusa/DAGent-t/pull/69**. Now let me complete the pipeline.

All done. Here's a summary of what was accomplished:  ## ✅ Draft PR Created Successfully  **PR #69:** [feat(commerce): add product quick view modal on PLP tiles](https://github.com/RomanKaliupinMelonusa/DAGent-t/pull/69)  ### PR Contents | Section | Details | |---|---| | **Executive Summary** | Quick View modal on PLP tiles — shoppers can preview products, select variants, and add to cart without navigating to PDP | | **4 source files** | `QuickViewModal` component, `ProductTile` override, + 2 test files (689 lines added) | | **Risk Score** | 2/10 — Additive feature, no base component modifications, uses PWA Kit override pattern | | **Testing** | 14+ unit tests covering modal behavior, a11y, loading/error states, and product type guards |  ### Pipeline Status - ✅ `pipeline:complete product-quick-view create-draft-pr` — marked done - ✅ Pipeline state committed and pushed

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git diff project/pwa-kit...HEAD -- apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/ | head -200
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git log --oneline project/pwa-kit...HEAD -- apps/commerce-storefront | head -20
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && gh pr list --head "$(git branch --show-current)" --json number,title,state 2>&1 || echo "NO_GH_CLI"

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git diff --numstat project/pwa-kit...HEAD -- apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/ | awk '{added+=$1; removed+=$2} END {print "Lines added:", added, "Lines removed:", removed}

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && gh pr create \ ↵ --draft \ ↵ --title "feat(commerce): add product quick view modal on PLP tiles" \ ↵ --body "## Executive Summary ↵ This PR enables shoppers to preview produ
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && npm run pipeline:complete product-quick-view create-draft-pr 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark create-draft-pr complete for product-quick-view" 2>&1
